response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned senior/staff engineer with 15+ years of experience, vastly overqualified for an entry-level junior Java role. Their core stack is JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang, with Java appearing only briefly years ago. Critically, German is listed as only 'Basic', which fails the 'Strong verbal and written communication in German' requirement for client-facing insurance consulting work. Seniority mismatch combined with the language gap make this a poor fit regardless of technical talent.
strengths: Deep software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, agile)
Strong polyglot OOP experience across Java, C#, TypeScript, DLang
Already based in Berlin with EU work history
concerns: Massive overqualification for an entry-level junior position
German listed as 'Basic' — fails fluency requirement for insurance clients
Limited recent Java focus; primary stack is JS/TS and DLang

key_factors.factor: Insufficient German proficiency for client work
